hypotheses.description: Mitigation systems (health & nutrition services, assistance) are broadly insufficient, inaccessible, overwhelmed, or non-functional
Mitigating systems are only partially functioning or partially accessible
Mitigating systems are generally functioning & accessible
Excess mortality (above the emergency threshold) is likely ongoing or imminent (next 3 months)
Conditions are severe and preventable deaths might be occurring or imminent, but not at a scale likely to lead to emergency levels
Conditions are not severe, or severe conditions appear sufficiently mitigated to make emergency levels of mortality unlikely now or in the next 3 months
Health outcomes are severe according to global standards and thresholds.
Health outcomes indicate substantial deterioration or elevated morbidity but are not currently at emergency levels according to global standards (e.g. comparable to IPC Phase 3).
Health outcomes are not currently severe (e.g., comparable to Phase 1 or 2).
Food consumption gaps are severe and widespread (system failure)
Food consumption gaps are severe but localized / affecting pockets.
Food consumption gaps are not severe or effectively mitigate.
Water services are severely compromised, affecting most of the population (system failure).
Water services are severely compromised, affecting pockets of the population.
Water services are not severely compromised or effectively mitigated.
Most people live in severely overcrowded, unsanitary, or unsafe conditions (system failure).
Severely overcrowded, unsanitary, or unsheltered conditions exist for some people, but are not widespread.
Living conditions are not severely inadequate or effectively mitigated.
Health / Nutrition services are severely compromised, affecting most of the population (system failure)
Health / Nutrition services are severely compromised, affecting pockets of the population
Health / Nutrition services are maintaining minimum service levels for most of the population, or severe gaps are mitigated
